task_completion.description: Start a Synthetic (algorithmic) session in OpenBCI GUI
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI should be running and display 'Synthetic' or 'Cyton' in the session/data source indicator at the top of the interface.

task_completion.description: Configure a 2-panel layout with Time Series and EMG widgets
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GUI workspace should be split into exactly two panels. One panel must display the 'Time Series' widget and the other must display the 'EMG' widget.
The Bandpass filter setting (usually found in the hardware/filter settings or top bar) should be set to '5-50 Hz'.
The Notch filter setting (usually near the Bandpass filter) should be set to 'None' or 'Off'.

task_completion.description: Set the Time Series Vertical Scale to 200 uV
task_completion.visual_evidence: The vertical scale setting within the Time Series widget (often a dropdown menu) should display '200 uV'.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the final configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: Evidence that a screenshot was saved to the specified path, such as a system notification, a terminal command, or a file manager showing 'emg_workspace.png' in '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenBCI GUI application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual OpenBCI GUI application being manipulated, rather than just terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: The settings are visible in the actual OpenBCI GUI interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The requested filters, scale, and widgets must be visible in the OpenBCI GUI interface, not just typed in a terminal or script.

integrity.description: The screenshot file was generated by the application or system screenshot tool
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use the GUI's built-in screenshot feature or a system screenshot tool to capture the workspace, rather than downloading or copying an existing image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct final state requires saving a screenshot to /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/emg_workspace.png. The GUI must display exactly two panels containing the 'Time Series' and 'EMG' widgets. The Bandpass filter must be set to '5-50 Hz' and Notch filter to 'None'. The vertical scale on the Time Series widget must be set to '200 uV'. The data source is specified as the built-in 'Synthetic (algorithmic)' session.
